c:Annotation.c:AnnotationText: Eurostat
Eurostat
Eurostat

s:Dataflow.c:Name: Personnes faisant l'objet de demandes d'asile en instance à la fin du mois par nationalité, âge et sexe - données mensuelles
Personen mit anhängigen Asylverfahren am Monatsende nach Staatsangehörigkeit, Alter und Geschlecht - monatliche Daten
Persons subject of asylum applications pending at the end of the month by citizenship, age and sex - monthly data

c:Annotation.c:AnnotationText: Standard code list
Eurostat Standard Code Lists
Not derived

s:Codelist.c:Name: Time frequency
Zeitliche Frequenz
Fréquence (relative au temps)
s:Codelist.c:Description: This code list contains the periodicity that refers to the frequency.

c:Annotation.c:AnnotationText: Standard code, follows Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Combined code list, contains standard codes and codes not associated with the concept of the code list
Eurostat Standard Code Lists
Not derived

s:Codelist.c:Name: Unit of measure
Maßeinheit
Unité de mesure
s:Codelist.c:Description: This code list specifies the standard measurement unit that is used to count inventoried items and express them in specific quantities.

c:Annotation.c:AnnotationText: Code not associated with the concept of the code list, but following Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Combined code list, contains standard codes and codes not associated with the concept of the code list
Eurostat Standard Code Lists

For demographic and migration statistics, ‘citizenship’ means the particular legal bond between an individual and his or her State, acquired by birth or naturalisation, whether by declaration, choice, marriage or other means according to national legislation. Citizenship is the status of a person recognized by a sovereign state as a member of its population (as a national), referring to some rights and obligations associated to that status. The person might have multiple citizenships. In the statistics, the citizenship of the person refers to the citizenship which is recognized by the reporting country. In the list of the citizenship there might be also reference to stateless persons (persons not recognized by any of the states) or unknown category (missing information about the concerned person).
Derived
Derived from geo code list

s:Codelist.c:Name: Country of citizenship
Land der Staatsangehörigkeit
Pays de nationalité
s:Codelist.c:Description: This code list contains the country of citizenship, the country in which a person is born (and has not renounced or lost citizenship) or naturalized whether by declaration, choice, marriage or other means according to national legislation, and to which that person owes allegiance and by which he or she is entitled to be protected.

The Holy See and the Vatican City State are separate subjects of international law. It is the Holy See, and not the Vatican City State, which is accredited to the European Union et al.

s:Code.c:Name: Holy See
Vatikanstadt
État de la Cité du Vatican

The United Arab Emirates is a constitutional federation of seven emirates: Abu Dhabi, Dubai, Ajman, Sharjah, Umm al-Qaywayn, Ra's al-Khaymah, and Al-Fujayrah.

s:Codelist.c:Name: Sex
Geschlecht
Sexe
s:Codelist.c:Description: This code list provides information about the state of being male or female and refers to their biological and physiological characteristics.

c:Annotation.c:AnnotationText: Standard code, follows Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Standard code, follows Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Standard code, follows Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Standard code, follows Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Combined code list, contains standard codes and codes not associated with the concept of the code list
Eurostat Standard Code Lists

The standard code list on age has been defined on the basis of the best practices used in the main domains supplying statistics according to the age breakdown (demography, Labour Force Survey, etc.), taking into account the 1982 UN document - Provisional guidelines on standard international age classifications. The code list covers single years of age, 5-year and 10-year age classes; it includes as well all the additional aggregates required for the needs of all statistical domains covered by the Reference database. In every age class the limits are included, unless explicitly stated; codes and labels are defined as follows: Y_LT15 Less than 15 years (i.e. 15 years excluded); Y_LE15 15 years or less (i.e. 15 years included); Y15-24 From 15 to 24 years (i.e. from 15 years included to 24 years included (i.e. less than 25)); Y_GT25 Over 25 years (i.e. 25 years excluded); Y_GE25 25 years or over (i.e. 25 years included)
Not derived

s:Codelist.c:Name: Age class
Altersklasse
Classe d'âge
s:Codelist.c:Description: This code list contains periods of time, i.e. the length of time that a person or an animal has lived or a plant or an entity related to human beings has existed. Age is usually measured in completed years, months, weeks, days, hours or minutes of life, as appropriate. Apart from that, the age of a means of transport is defined as: Length of time after the first registration of a means of transport as new.

c:Annotation.c:AnnotationText: Standard code, follows Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Standard code, follows Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Standard code, follows Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Standard code, follows Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Standard code, follows Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Standard code, follows Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Standard code, follows Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Standard code, follows Eurostat Standard Code List Guidelines
Combined code list, contains standard codes and codes not associated with the concept of the code list
Eurostat Standard Code Lists

The geographical standard code list provides codes on geographical entities at the level of aggregates, countries, regions and organizations; the labels are available in EN, DE and FR and they are in line with the EU Interinstitutional Style Guide. The work is based on various international sources: Interinstitutional Style Guide, ISO 3166 country codes (3166-1 alpha-2), UN standard country and area codes classification. The structure follows the UN standard country or area code classification (M49) and adopts in most of the cases the proposed geographical order. In case of conflict, the most appropriate solution in the interest of the ESS has been chosen. The country codes correspond to the ISO 3166 classification with the exception of: United Kingdom where the code UK was adopted (instead of GB) and Greece where the code EL was adopted (instead of GR) in order to comply with the Interinstitutional Style Guide. The presentation of the code list has been enhanced with additional information on the composition of the aggregates and on the dependency status of various geographical entities (mainly overseas territories). ISO 3166-1-alpha-2 - ISO 3166 International Standard for country codes; NUTS
Not derived

s:Codelist.c:Name: Geopolitical entity (reporting)
Geopolitische Meldeeinheit
Entité géopolitique (déclarante)
s:Codelist.c:Description: This code list defines the reporting geopolitical entities.
